You deserve respect, and are encouraged to practice self-care so that
you can remain focused and engaged; that might mean getting a drink of
water, leaving to use the restroom, taking a moment to stretch, or doing
something else you need to do to take care of yourself. Please be
respectful of others by minimizing distractions when practicing
self-care.

College life can be overwhelming at times, but you are not alone if
you're feeling stressed. For many of us, systems of oppression such as
racism, sexism, heterosexism or cissexism may cause additional stress.
Please remember to practice self-care and reach out for support when you
need it.

You can visit the [Aggie Compass Basic Needs
Center](https://aggiecompass.ucdavis.edu/) to find resources related to
return to campus, health and well-being, academics, basic needs (food
and housing) and more.

We also encourage you to explore some of the resources available for
graduate students on campus:

- The **GradPathways Institute for Professional Development**:
  <https://grad.ucdavis.edu/professional-development>
